steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures on each side of the blank
steps.content: Left side: independent clause ending “...via the magazine”. Right side: participial phrase starting with “Published” and running through the list of cities, followed by the independent clause “this magazine helped...”

steps.title: Apply the sentence boundary rule
steps.content:
Choice C places a period after Belgravia, properly ending the first independent clause and allowing the second sentence to begin with the participial phrase “Published from 1866 to 1899 and distributed throughout [cities],” followed by the main clause “this magazine helped...” Choices A and D fail to establish a proper sentence boundary and leave the comma splice, while choice B misuses a semicolon before a non-independent phrase.

Two independent clauses must be separated by a period, semicolon, or coordinating conjunction.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
